details: Structured data may enter without trusted framework metadata. Bootstrap is relevant when the workflow chooses to supply missing intent after that boundary crossing.

Bootstrap supplies required transformation intent, default metadata, presentation defaults, validation defaults, workbook-view defaults, or schema conventions. Fill-if-absent behavior is lifecycle-relevant because readback/user-carried metadata may remain effective.

Runtime resolution and projection-scope artifacts may be needed until inverse recomposition has consumed them. If a projection cannot consume all such artifacts itself, a workflow-owned prune step may remove stale or non-durable metadata before structured persistence.

FK helper materialization depends on FK relation intent. The inquiry treats missing required intent as a named producer/configuration problem rather than something the consuming step should silently guess.

FK helpers are a forward projection support surface that makes references more human-readable during workbook editing. Their lifecycle depends on later cleanup so helper values do not become canonical payload by accident.

Helper cleanup belongs after the user-facing projection has been read back and interpreted. The lifecycle note is draft because the inquiry sources identify helper cleanup pressure but do not approve a complete helper contract here.
Derived helper columns should not silently persist as durable structured payload. Cleanup before structured persistence keeps the forward projection support surface separate from canonical data.
Cell Codec compacts several structured attributes into one human-editable cell value for workbook readability. The structured attributes remain the canonical representation.

Compact values are presentation-oriented but must remain parseable for the inverse path. The inquiry emphasizes reversibility and explicit codec intent rather than treating compact strings as independent canonical data.
Expand Codec reverses the compact cell representation back into structured attributes. The draft contract depends on configured encoding strategy and deterministic roundtrip behavior.
XRef Crosstable projects a durable tuple relation into a human-friendly editable matrix. The matrix is a scoped editing representation, not the durable source structure.

Projection/writeback scope may be needed to distinguish blank cells from missing rows, missing columns, damaged projection structure, or deleted axis entities. Resolved XRef information may be scope evidence, but not durable canonical truth.

The inverse transformation interprets present addressable matrix cells as updates inside the configured XRef scope. Out-of-scope relation rows are preserved and no merge heuristic should guess ownership outside the configured scope.

Missing rows or columns produce no update statement in the baseline, while present blank cells remove scoped relation entries. Header tampering and projection-shape integrity are cross-cutting readback risks, not baseline XRef semantics.

Existence-only relations need a visible matrix value even when the relation carries no business payload. The extension acts as a support projection so XRef Crosstable can render and edit relation existence in cell form.

Dropping the artificial existence value returns an existence-only XRef to pair-like relation semantics. The lifecycle risk is that this is intentionally lossy for any real cell payload and should only apply to existence markers.

Future conditional views describe filtered workbook surfaces produced after a prior classification roundtrip. This suggests discriminator-style splitting may be a supporting projection pattern, but the inquiry does not approve a concrete split-by-discriminator contract.

Derived specialized views may overlap or become irrelevant after classification changes. The inquiry calls for explicit identity, merge, and relevance-loss policies before trusting such surfaces for roundtrip maintenance.

Future conditional views merge edited specialized views back into full detail data by stable identity. This is relevant to merge-by-discriminator, but remains a draft inference rather than an approved discriminator merge rule.
Legend entries must be resolved from a durable home outside stale metadata, either structured data or pipeline configuration. A prior _meta value is not trusted as the durable source of legend truth.

Add Legend contributes workbook/projection intent: validation/dropdowns, optional visible support legend, and value context for payload cells. The rendered legend surface is a support projection, not canonical payload.
The renderer may create a visible legend table and concrete workbook placement from legend intent. Placement and reconstruction hints are render/readback support artifacts, not durable model truth.

Generated legend tables must remain distinguishable from payload on readback. Workbook reconstruction hints may help the parser classify support surfaces but should not become sidecar-durable metadata.
Drop Legend removes or classifies generated legend/support surfaces so they do not reimport as ordinary payload frames. Edited payload cells are preserved separately from generated legend tables.

Legend reconstruction hints and parser-local residue should be pruned before structured sidecar persistence. Durable legend entries and usage intent survive through their model/configuration home instead.
